Title: Pumping iron: A multi-omics analysis of two extremophilic algae reveals iron economy management
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: TFN1; TFN2; other transferrin-family genes (expanded Tf family)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron deficiency (-Fe) versus iron replete (+Fe) growth media
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Many Tf genes/proteins are highly expressed and dramatically up-regulated under -Fe; proteins with conserved Fe-binding residues show strongest induction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell surface/plasma membrane-associated Fe uptake interface
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dunaliella contains a major expansion of transferrin-family genes, with seven in D. tertiolecta and six in D. salina Bardawil. 2. Many Tf proteins are strongly induced by iron deficiency and are inferred to mediate high-affinity ferric iron uptake. 3. Dunaliella Tf proteins are membrane-bound, unlike soluble metazoan transferrins, suggesting dual Fe binding and internalization functions.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transferrin-family glycoproteins with 1-10 Tf domains, transmembrane domain, signal peptide, glycosylation sites
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely energetically costly because high quantities of Tf protein must be produced
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; RNA-seq; proteomics; domain conservation analysis; structure prediction; prior biochemical association evidence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance algal iron acquisition and productivity in low-iron environments
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron supports photosynthesis and growth under iron-poor environments
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes iron acquisition capacity under low-iron conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Some divergent Tf proteins lacking Fe-binding residues may be pseudogenes or may have evolved novel functions; TFN1 structure predicted as three-domain protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong induction in both species under -Fe

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All SUPT genes are strongly up-regulated in response to Fe limitation; proteins also accumulate under -Fe except D. salina Bardawil SUPT1 due to nonsense mutation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A p130B-like protein family was identified and renamed SUPT. 2. All five SUPT-family genes are strongly induced by iron deficiency. 3. Sequence/structural similarity to diatom ISIP1 supports a role in siderophore-bound iron uptake.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cell-surface glycoproteins with signal peptide, C-terminal transmembrane helix, glycosylation sites, twin seven-bladed beta-propeller domains
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; proteomics; structure prediction; similarity network analysis; comparative genomics
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate genes for engineering siderophore-dependent iron capture in algae
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enables utilization of siderophore-bound iron in iron-poor habitats
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes uptake of siderophore-bound Fe under iron deficiency
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: D. salina Bardawil SUPT1 carries a recent nonsense mutation; no endogenous siderophore biosynthetic pathway was found, implying uptake of exogenous siderophore-bound Fe
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in both species under -Fe

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed exclusively or very strongly under Fe-starvation conditions; highly up-regulated in both species
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TIDI1 is a distinct LHCA3-related chlorophyll-binding protein strongly induced by iron deficiency. 2. Its induction accompanies photosystem remodeling that favors lower iron demand. 3. It is proposed to act as an accessory antenna associated with PSI under Fe starvation.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LHCA3-like chlorophyll a/b-binding protein with characteristic proline-rich N terminus and PFxGxxPF-containing loop between helices B and C
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with photosystem remodeling under low Fe; exact impact on photosynthetic efficiency not fully resolved
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; proteomics; protein similarity network; structure prediction; comparative genomics
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering photosynthetic resilience under low-iron conditions
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports maintenance of photosynthesis with reduced cellular Fe quota
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with acclimation of photosynthesis to iron deficiency
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from canonical LHCA3 and absent from Chlamydomonas reinhardtii and land plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in both species in -Fe

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FER1 is down-regulated under iron deficiency. 2. Reduced ferritin abundance is consistent with release of stored iron when Fe is scarce.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to modulate iron storage and remobilization
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron storage and release for homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely decreases iron storage and facilitates remobilization under low Fe

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FOX1 is a core component of the reductive iron uptake pathway and is strongly induced in low Fe. 2. It may participate in reductive uptake and has been observed associated with transferrin-related components.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; proteomics; prior biochemical association evidence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to improve iron assimilation in algae
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cellular acquisition of iron for metabolism and photosynthesis

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FRE1A is induced under iron deficiency as part of the reductive iron uptake pathway. 2. It contributes to acquisition of iron when external iron is limiting.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for improved reductive iron uptake
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports low-iron acclimation through reductive uptake

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in +Fe; FDX1A dominant; dramatically down-regulated in -Fe
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FDX1 genes encode iron-sulfur ferredoxins that are abundant in iron-replete cells. 2. Their down-regulation under low Fe contributes to iron sparing. 3. Replacement of FDX1A with FLD1 frees substantial cellular iron.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Replacement may reduce electron transfer rates
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; proteomics; SRM quantification for FDX1A
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be paired with FLD1 to optimize iron economy in engineered algae
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Electron transfer in photosynthetic metabolism but also a major cellular iron sink
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulation reduces iron demand under deficiency
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulation differs between species: FDX1A downregulated transcriptionally in D. tertiolecta but mainly at protein level in D. salina Bardawil
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in +Fe and strongly reduced in -Fe

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increased expression under poor Fe nutrition
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NRAMP4 is part of the Fe-responsive protein set shared with the green lineage. 2. It likely contributes to iron transport during deficiency.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for iron transport engineering
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metal transport during iron deficiency acclimation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes iron mobilization/transport under low Fe

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; proteomics; SRM quantification; metabolite profiling of flavins
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Photosynthetic electron transfer / iron-sparing substitution linked to flavin metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for replacement of Fe-rich electron carrier with flavin-based substitute
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cellular iron quota / FMN-dependent flavodoxin function
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a flavin-based electron carrier replacing ferredoxin under low iron rather than catalyzing a classical small-molecule conversion
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May have reduced electron transfer rates compared with ferredoxin; substantial draw on flavin cofactor pool
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLD1 is strongly induced under low Fe while ferredoxins are repressed. 2. FLD1 replaces FDX1A to spare iron. 3. This switch frees ~1×10^8 Fe atoms per D. salina Bardawil cell and is coupled to increased FMN production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Counterregulated with FDX1 genes; strongly induced in -Fe
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly increased in -Fe in both species
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in iron-sparing response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes iron sparing by replacing Fe-containing ferredoxin with FMN-containing flavodoxin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Absent from plants and Chlamydomonas but retained in Dunaliella

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; pathway analysis; flavin metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dual-function enzyme initiating the flavin biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RIBBA1 expression increases under iron deficiency. 2. Its induction supports elevated flavin synthesis needed for FLD1 accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in D. salina Bardawil under -Fe
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports increased flavin biosynthesis during iron sparing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as a key induced enzyme when FMN increased >6-fold

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; pathway analysis; flavin metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RFS1 is induced under iron deficiency. 2. It contributes to increased riboflavin/FMN synthesis supporting FLD1-based iron sparing.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in D. salina Bardawil under -Fe
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavin accumulation associated with low-iron acclimation
